Policy-Relevant Inflation (PCE) Signal
← Back to dashboard

Policy-Relevant Inflation (PCE) Signal

Policy-relevant inflation: composite of headline and core PCE.

As of 2036-10-01

Gemini Summary

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement: Given a composite score of -0.232 and aligned headline/core z-scores, this setup aligns with Indeterminate price paths and Compressed volatility, where the dominant risk is Data uncertainty, not Regime shift.
  • The signal is currently in a persistent Neutral regime, characterized by inflation levels approximately 0.9-1.0 percentage points above the 2% policy target (1).
  • Conviction Band: Medium. Interpretation Confidence: High Confidence. Internal Conflict Flag: No. Signal Stability Assessment: Stable. Threshold Proximity: Far. Revision Sensitivity: Moderate.

Methodology Applied:

  • Standardization: Headline (PCEPI) and Core (PCEPILFE) YoY rates are converted to rolling robust z-scores to reduce outlier sensitivity (1).
  • Composite Construction: The signal score is an equal-weighted average (50/50) of the headline and core robust z-scores (1).
  • Regime Mapping: Thresholds are set at +/- 0.75 z-score units; values between these bounds are classified as Neutral (1).
  • Dataset: Policy-Relevant Inflation (PCE) Signal; latest observation: 2025-12-31.

Key Dynamics:

  • The primary driver is the stabilization of Core PCE YoY at 2.99% and Headline PCE YoY at 2.90%, yielding near-zero z-scores (1).
  • Internal Coherence: High; both components show similar downward normalization from 2023 "Hot" peaks toward the Neutral zone.
  • Conditional Invalidation: A composite score move above +0.75 or below -0.75 would trigger a regime shift to Hot or Cool, respectively (1).
  • Persistence: The Neutral regime has been maintained for 28 consecutive months since September 2023.

Scenario Balance:

  • Base case dominant: Continued Neutral regime as inflation remains sticky but non-accelerating above target.
  • Upside risk: Re-entry into "Hot" regime triggered by a commodity price shock or core services acceleration.
  • Downside risk: Shift to "Cool" regime triggered by a rapid contraction in consumer demand.

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Time Horizon: Cyclical (months). Inflation trends reflect medium-term policy impacts and demand cycles.
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: High. PCE is the Federal Reserve's preferred gauge for its 2% objective (1).

Macro Relevance:

  • Informs the pricing and inflation dimension, specifically the Federal Reserve's policy anchor.
  • Economic mechanism: Measures distance from the 2% price stability target to infer potential for policy adjustment (1).
  • Cycle position: Not determined.
  • Typically interacts with Labor Market and Liquidity signals to define the "Fed Watcher" decision matrix (2).

Regime Context:

  • Regime is highly persistent, having transitioned from "Hot" in mid-2023 to "Neutral" and remaining range-bound thereafter.
  • Direction of change: Stabilizing.

Model Limitations:

  • BEA updates and benchmark revisions can significantly alter the latest year of data history (1).
  • Equal weighting of headline and core may mask underlying sectoral divergences.

Data & References:

  • Policy-Relevant Inflation (PCE) Signal (2025-12-31) (1).
  • Influential Datapoints: Core PCE YoY (2.99%) and Composite Score (-0.23).
  • Supplementary Data: Dallas Fed Trimmed Mean PCE and ALFRED vintage comparisons would enhance revision tracking.

Policy-Relevant Inflation (PCE) Chart

Equal-weight composite of headline and core PCE, scaled into a policy-relevant inflation index.
